## Throughput matrix — cross-machine speedup (babo / liqui)

## Scaling curve — cancel latency vs resting-book size

Speedup (liqui ns ÷ babo ns) per resting-book size N. babo is O(1) (flat); liquibook is O(depth) (climbs). The gap widens with N — the mechanism, not a single machine's constant.

### Cancel speedup by N

| Resting N | Darwin-Apple M4 Pro |
|---:|---:|
| 1,000 | 2.7× |
| 2,000 | 3.6× |
| 5,000 | 6.3× |
| 10,000 | 12.8× |
| 20,000 | 23.4× |
| 50,000 | 62.7× |
| 100,000 | 122.6× |
| 200,000 | 161.7× |

### Shape confirmation (ns/cancel range, smallest→largest N)

| Label | babo ns (min→max) | liqui ns (min→max) | max speedup |
|---|---|---|---:|
| Darwin-Apple M4 Pro | 22.7 → 44.8 | 61 → 7243 | 161.7× |
